# Rule Catalog

| Implemented | 149/156 |

| Severity fallback (medium default) | 0/156 |

## Rule ID Conventions

Rule IDs identify the **type of check**, not the validator that runs it.
The prefix is orthogonal to severity — see [ADR-043](../../.sdlc/adrs/ADR-043-default-severity-assignment.md).

| Prefix | Category | ID ranges |
|--------|----------|-----------|
| **L** | Lint (style, correctness, best practice) | L001–L199 |
| **M** | Modernize (ansible-core migration) | M001–M099 |
| **R** | Risk / security (annotation-based) | R001–R499 |
| **P** | Policy (legacy runtime validation) | P001–P099 |
| **A** | AAP-specific (platform compatibility) | A001–A099 |
| **SEC:** | Secrets (Gitleaks) | SEC:* |

For ansible-lint name cross-references and historical renumbering, see
[LINT_RULE_MAPPING.md](LINT_RULE_MAPPING.md).

## Severity Scale (ADR-043)

Default severity is assigned from `src/apme_engine/severity_defaults.py` using this decision tree:

1. Security vulnerability? → **critical**
2. Runtime breakage today? → **error**
3. Imminent breakage or risky behavior? → **high**
4. Probably a bug or anti-pattern? → **medium**
5. Best practice / convention? → **low**
6. Informational / style? → **info**

Category prefix does **not** determine severity. For example, L057 (syntax) is **error**
while L060 (line length) is **info** — both are L-rules.

## Remediation Tiers

Routing follows `src/apme_engine/remediation/partition.py` (ADR-026 scope metadata):

| Tier | Label | Count | Routing |
|------|-------|-------|---------|
| 1 | auto | 25 | Deterministic transform in registry — applied by `apme remediate` |
| 2 | ai | 69 | Task/block scope, no fixer — AI proposes patch (Abbenay) |
| 3 | manual | 62 | Play/role/collection scope, cross-file, or info severity |

Full analysis and promotion candidates: [REMEDIATION_TIER_REPORT.md](REMEDIATION_TIER_REPORT.md).

## Fixer Summary (Tier 1)

Deterministic fixers are auto-applied by `apme remediate`.

| Rule ID | Severity | Scope | Description |
|---------|----------|-------|-------------|
| L005 | low | task | Community collection module detected; use certified or validated collections. |
| L007 | low | task | Prefer command when no shell features needed. |
| L008 | low | task | Use delegate_to: localhost instead of local_action. |
| L009 | medium | task | Avoid comparison to empty string in when. |
| L010 | medium | task | Use failed_when or register instead of ignore_errors. |
| L011 | low | task | Avoid literal true/false in when. |
| L012 | low | task | Avoid state=latest; pin package versions. |
| L013 | medium | task | command/shell/raw need changed_when or creates/removes. |
| L015 | low | task | Avoid Jinja in when; use variables. |
| L018 | high | play | become_user should have corresponding become. |
| L020 | high | task | mode should be string with leading zero. |
| L021 | low | task | Set mode explicitly for file/copy/template. |
| L022 | low | task | Shell with pipe should set set -o pipefail. |
| L025 | low | task | Task/play name should start with uppercase. |
| L026 | low | task | Tasks should use FQCN for modules. |
| L043 | low | task | Bare variable in with_* loop directive; use {{ var }}. |
| L046 | low | task | Avoid raw/command/shell without args key. |
| L084 | low | task | Task names in included sub-task files should use a prefix. |
| M001 | high | task | FQCN resolution — module resolved to a different canonical name. |
| M002 | high | task | Deprecated module — module has deprecation metadata. |
| M003 | high | task | Module redirect — module name was redirected to a new FQCN. |
| M004 | error | task | Removed module — tombstoned module that raises AnsiblePluginRemovedError. |
| M006 | high | task | become with ignore_errors will not catch timeout in 2.19+. |
| M008 | high | task | Bare include is removed in 2.19+; use include_tasks or import_tasks. |
| M009 | high | task | with_* loops are deprecated; use loop instead. |
